params.name: CidrBlock
params.description: The IPv4 network range for the VPC in CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.0.0/16)

params.name: AmazonProvidedIpv6CidrBlock
params.description: Requests an Amazon-provided IPv6 CIDR block with a /56 prefix length

params.name: InstanceTenancy
params.description: The tenancy options for instances launched into the VPC

items.docs: Creates a VPC with the specified IPv4 CIDR block. The smallest VPC you can create uses a /28 netmask (16 IPv4 addresses), and the largest uses a /16 netmask (65,536 IPv4 addresses). After creating the VPC, you can add one or more subnets.

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The IDs of the VPCs to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes one or more of your VPCs. Returns information about the VPC including the CIDR block, DHCP options set, state, and tags.

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC to delete

items.docs: Deletes the specified VPC. You must detach or delete all gateways and resources that are associated with the VPC before you can delete it. You must also delete all subnets associated with the VPC.

info.name: Amazon VPC Modify a VPC Attribute

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC

params.name: EnableDnsSupport.Value
params.description: Indicates whether DNS resolution is supported for the VPC

params.name: EnableDnsHostnames.Value
params.description: Indicates whether instances launched in the VPC get DNS hostnames

items.docs: Modifies the specified attribute of the specified VPC. You can modify the DNS resolution and DNS hostname attributes.

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC

params.name: CidrBlock
params.description: The IPv4 network range for the subnet in CIDR notation

params.name: AvailabilityZone
params.description: The Availability Zone or Local Zone for the subnet

params.name: Ipv6CidrBlock
params.description: The IPv6 network range for the subnet in CIDR notation

items.docs: Creates a subnet in the specified VPC. The new subnet must have a CIDR block that is within the VPC CIDR block. You can optionally specify an Availability Zone in which to create the subnet.

params.name: SubnetId
params.description: The IDs of the subnets to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes one or more of your subnets. Returns information about the subnet including the CIDR block, Availability Zone, VPC, and state.

params.name: SubnetId
params.description: The ID of the subnet to delete

items.docs: Deletes the specified subnet. You must terminate all running instances in the subnet before you can delete the subnet.

info.name: Amazon VPC Create an Internet Gateway

items.docs: Creates an internet gateway for use with a VPC. After creating the internet gateway, you attach it to a VPC using AttachInternetGateway.

info.name: Amazon VPC Attach an Internet Gateway to a VPC

params.name: InternetGatewayId
params.description: The ID of the internet gateway

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC

items.docs: Attaches an internet gateway or a virtual private gateway to a VPC, enabling connectivity between the internet and the VPC.

info.name: Amazon VPC Create a NAT Gateway

params.name: SubnetId
params.description: The ID of the subnet in which to create the NAT gateway

params.name: AllocationId
params.description: The allocation ID of an Elastic IP address to associate with the NAT gateway

params.name: ConnectivityType
params.description: Indicates whether the NAT gateway supports public or private connectivity

items.docs: Creates a NAT gateway in the specified subnet. A NAT gateway enables instances in a private subnet to connect to the internet or other AWS services, but prevents the internet from initiating a connection with those instances.

params.name: NatGatewayId
params.description: The IDs of the NAT gateways to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes one or more of your NAT gateways. Returns information about the NAT gateway including its state, subnet, and associated Elastic IP address.

info.name: Amazon VPC Create a Route Table

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC

items.docs: Creates a route table for the specified VPC. After you create a route table, you can add routes and associate the table with a subnet.

params.name: RouteTableId
params.description: The IDs of the route tables to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes one or more of your route tables. Each subnet in your VPC must be associated with a route table, which controls the routing for the subnet.

info.name: Amazon VPC Create a Route in a Route Table

params.name: RouteTableId
params.description: The ID of the route table for the route

params.name: DestinationCidrBlock
params.description: The IPv4 CIDR address block used for the destination match

params.name: GatewayId
params.description: The ID of an internet gateway or virtual private gateway

params.name: NatGatewayId
params.description: The ID of a NAT gateway

params.name: NetworkInterfaceId
params.description: The ID of a network interface

items.docs: Creates a route in a route table within a VPC. You must specify either a destination CIDR block or a prefix list ID. You must also specify exactly one of the targets.

info.name: Amazon VPC Associate a Route Table with a Subnet

params.name: RouteTableId
params.description: The ID of the route table

params.name: SubnetId
params.description: The ID of the subnet

params.name: GatewayId
params.description: The ID of the internet gateway or virtual private gateway

items.docs: Associates a subnet or gateway with a route table. The subnet and route table must be in the same VPC. This association causes traffic from the subnet to be routed according to the routes in the route table.

info.name: Amazon VPC Create a Network ACL

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC

items.docs: Creates a network ACL in a VPC. Network ACLs provide an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets.

params.name: NetworkAclId
params.description: The IDs of the network ACLs to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes one or more of your network ACLs. A network ACL is an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets.
